content:
🚀 nyx | Shell
🎯 Primary Use Case: Cleaning forensic traces and sensitive data from operating systems to enhance privacy and security.
✨ Key Features: • Cross-platform support (Linux, macOS, Windows) • Comprehensive artifact cleaning (shell history, logs, network, user data, browsers, applications) • Modular design for targeted cleaning • Self-contained script for ease of use • Alpha software with ongoing development
📖 Summary: Nyx is a self-contained shell script designed to clean forensic traces across Linux, macOS, and Windows operating systems. It systematically removes artifacts related to shell history, logs, network activity, user data, browsers, and various applications to help maintain system privacy and security.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 ShizuWall | Kotlin
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide users with granular control over application network access on Android devices without requiring root privileges or a VPN connection, enhancing privacy and reducing data usage.
✨ Key Features: • No root or VPN required • Per-app system networking control • Privacy-first design • Leverages Shizuku or local ADB daemon
📖 Summary: ShizuWall is a lightweight Android firewall application that allows users to block network connections for specific apps without needing root access or a VPN. It utilizes the Shizuku service or a local ADB daemon to manage network permissions on a per-app basis, offering a privacy-focused solution.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 dline | Shell
🎯 Primary Use Case: Managing personal schedules and important dates directly within the terminal environment.
✨ Key Features: • Dynamic calendar view with color-coded events and filtering • Static monthly calendar view • Event calculator for workdays and timespans • Calendar data management (add, delete, update, import, export) • Support for multiple calendars and public holidays
📖 Summary: dLine is a command-line calendar tool designed for terminal enthusiasts. It allows users to view monthly calendars, track events, calculate timespans between dates, and manage multiple calendar datasets directly from their terminal.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 sablier | Go
🎯 Primary Use Case: To reduce resource consumption and operational costs by automatically managing the lifecycle of containerized applications, ensuring they are only active when actively being used.
✨ Key Features: • Starts containers on demand • Automatically shuts down inactive containers • Docker, Docker Swarm Mode, and Kubernetes compatible
📖 Summary: Sablier is an open-source tool designed to manage containerized workloads by starting them only when needed and automatically shutting them down after a period of inactivity. It integrates with various reverse proxies and orchestration platforms like Docker, Docker Swarm, and Kubernetes to optimize resource usage.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source
